userMessage: Set CH1 200mV DC 50ohm VDD_CORE, CH2 500mV DC 50ohm VDD_IO, CH3 1V DC 50ohm VDD_PHY, CH4 2V DC 1Mohm PGOOD signal. Set trigger on CH4 rising edge 1V normal mode. Single sequence acquisition record length 10 million samples. Add measurements: delay from CH4 rising to CH1 crossing 100mV, delay from CH4 rising to CH2 crossing 250mV, delay from CH4 rising to CH3 crossing 500mV. Add pk2pk and mean on CH1 CH2 CH3. Query all delay and measurement results saving to named variables. Save all 4 channels as binary files. Take screenshot.

response.text:
I can’t safely emit applyable TekAutomate flow JSON for this request.

Verified this turn:
- Channel scale, coupling, termination, label
- Trigger edge source, slope, mode
- Trigger level via `TRIGger:{A|B}:LEVel:CH<x> <NR1>`
- Single-sequence acquisition via `ACQuire:STOPAfter` and `ACQuire:STATE`
- Record length via `HORizontal:RECOrdlength`
- Measurement add via `MEASUrement:ADDMEAS`
- Some measurement edge/result query headers
- `*OPC?`
- Built-in `save_waveform` and `save_screenshot` steps are safe

Not sufficiently verified this turn:
- Exact dual-source setup for delay measurements on MSO4/5/6 (`SOURCE1` / `SOURCE2` or equivalent)
- Exact absolute crossing/reference-level commands needed for 100 mV / 250 mV / 500 mV delay thresholds

Because those are required to implement your three delay measurements correctly, I’m failing closed rather than guessing.

If you want, next turn I can build the full flow as soon as those exact measurement-source and reference-threshold SCPI headers are provided or retrieved.
